reforms.description: Austria introduced tax concessions for research and development to enhance the attractiveness of the country as a business location and to promote innovation. Additionally, a tax reform aimed at lowering the effective tax rate on equity is expected to stimulate venture capital markets and encourage investment in innovative enterprises.
reforms.package_name: Tax Incentives for R&D and Innovation
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax concessions and reforms are designed to attract investment and stimulate innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully implemented as of 1999.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant changes in the tax structure aimed at enhancing innovation and investment.
reforms.source_quote: Tax concessions for research and development aim at fostering the attractiveness of Austria as a business location and at promoting employment.

reforms.description: The Austrian government has launched a 'technology offensive' aimed at improving the innovation system by establishing competence centres and increasing R&D spending. This initiative addresses low R&D expenditures and deficiencies in the innovation system, supporting research networks involving research institutions and enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing R&D spending and establishing competence centres, the initiative aims to enhance the innovation ecosystem, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The technology offensive has been implemented successfully as of 1999.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a major structural change in the approach to R&D funding and innovation support.
reforms.source_quote: In response, the government has launched a 'technology offensive' designed to address the problem with new programmes (e.g. to establish competence centres) and to raise the level of R&D spending.

reforms.description: The Austrian government and social partners agreed on a National Action Plan for Employment, which includes fostering research and innovation to increase employment levels. A technology package is being implemented to raise the level of R&D in Austria, promoting clusters in basic research and subsidizing the employment of scientists in industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The focus on research and innovation is expected to create jobs and enhance the overall economic environment, contributing to growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives under the National Action Plan have been implemented successfully as of 1998.
reforms.importance_rationale: The agreement represents a significant policy shift towards integrating research and innovation into employment strategies.
reforms.source_quote: Major elements of the National Action Plan are: ... fostering research and innovation for the purpose of increasing the level of employment.

reforms.description: The Austrian government has implemented various measures to support the startup ecosystem, including extending unemployment benefits for self-employed individuals, proposing a 'one-stop-shop' for business approvals, and remodeling public support programmes to encourage venture capital funds. Additionally, the Industrial Research Promotion Fund has been established to facilitate joint programmes between polytechnics and enterprises, and competence centres are being created to integrate university and industry collaboration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures are designed to enhance the startup ecosystem, reduce barriers to entrepreneurship, and facilitate knowledge transfer between academia and industry, all of which contribute to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The various measures have been successfully implemented as of 1999.
reforms.importance_rationale: While these measures are significant, they are more incremental adjustments rather than major structural reforms.
reforms.source_quote: The government has recently extended coverage so that individuals with a right to unemployment benefits will preserve their entitlement during a period of self-employment in case the enterprise fails.
